content: A 2025 meta-learning memory consolidation agent can reframe its own core agent design by treating incremental unprocessed search findings as short-term memory fragments that need to be consolidated, rather than treating agent architecture as a fixed static blueprint. This lets the agent iteratively prune redundant design assumptions (like repeated identical search-derived agent definitions) and re-prioritize high-value structural features that improve active learning and curiosity-driven exploration over time.
